Why Kranus Mictera

Take Back Control of Your Bladder Without Medication

Clinically validated pelvic floor and urgency training — delivered through a simple, private app that fits into your daily life.

📓

Voiding Diary

Record fluid intake, urine output, and urgency episodes to track patterns and monitor progress over time.

🏋️

Pelvic Floor & Bladder Training

Guided exercises and physiotherapy components designed to build lasting control and reduce urgency.

🧘

Relaxation & Mental Exercises

Stress-reduction and mindfulness techniques plus urge-control strategies to address the mental dimension.

📊

Real-Time Progress Tracking

Graphic analyses of your improvement so you always know how far you've come.

How It Works

Start Your Therapy in 4 Simple Steps

Getting started is straightforward — your insurance covers everything.

1
Get Prescribed by Your Doctor
Ask your urologist or GP for the Kranus Mictera PZN prescription code.
PZN: 20287989
2
Upload Your Prescription
Upload your prescription securely on the Kranus Health website in minutes.
3
Receive Your Activation Code
Your insurance company sends your personal activation code by mail — fully covered.
4
Start Your 12-Week Program
Enter your code in the app and begin your training. A follow-up prescription is possible after 12 weeks.

Built on Trust, Certified for Safety

Kranus Mictera meets the highest standards in digital health and data privacy.

BfArM Certified
Data protection and security meet the strict requirements of the Federal Institute for Drugs and Medical Devices (BfArM), exceeding standard GDPR compliance.
Insurance Covered
Fully reimbursed by statutory health insurance — no impact on your prescription drug budget.
Efficacy Validated by DINKS Study
92% of participants experienced improvements in their symptoms. Conducted by Prof. Dr. med. Alex Haferkamp (University Hospital Mainz) with 194 participants, the study confirms Mictera's clinical effectiveness.

FAQ

Your Questions, Answered

Everything you need to know before getting started.

What is Kranus Mictera?
▾
Kranus Mictera helps women with urinary incontinence improve their symptoms and increase quality of life. The goal is to reduce incontinence episodes through a holistic, app-based therapy. It was developed by experts in women's health, is scientifically sound, and is based on current medical guidelines.
What does the Kranus Mictera app include?
▾
The 12-week therapy follows a multimodal approach. It includes a voiding diary to record fluid intake and urine output, integrated bladder training and pelvic floor exercises with physiotherapy components, mental relaxation techniques to reduce stress, an educational section on bladder function, bladder-friendly diet tips with recipe suggestions, and real-time personal progress tracking with graphic analysis.
How do I get a prescription for Mictera?
▾
Simply ask your GP, urologist, or gynecologist for a prescription using the Kranus Mictera PZN code. Most doctors are already familiar with the program and it takes less than a minute to prescribe.

PZN 20287989

Approved indications:
· Stress incontinence (ICD N39.3)
· Urge incontinence (ICD N39.42)
How secure is my personal health data?
▾
Your data security is our highest priority. All data is processed within the European Union in full compliance with GDPR, the Medical Devices Regulation, and the Medical Devices Implementation Act. Data is transmitted to our servers exclusively in encrypted form and stored encrypted. Your profile is additionally encrypted — third-party access to this data is technically impossible.
Can I use it alongside other treatments?
▾
Yes, Kranus Mictera can be used as a standalone therapy or alongside other treatments recommended by your doctor. Always consult your healthcare provider for personalised medical advice.
What is a Digital Health Application (DiGA)?
▾
Digital Health Applications (DiGA) are CE-marked medical devices — such as health apps — that assist in the treatment of illnesses. They are thoroughly reviewed and approved by the Federal Institute for Drugs and Medical Devices (BfArM) and reimbursed by health insurance companies. Kranus Mictera is a fully approved DiGA.
